title: The Film Historian Who’s Sick of Movies
description: After a lifetime spent reviewing them, David Thomson makes a provocative but unconvincing case.
author: Michael O’Donnell

title: How to Survive This Heat
description: Whether you’re playing in the World Cup or just trying to get through the day
author: Nancy Walecki

title: Pete Buttigieg’s Ordeal Is a Frightening New Form of Political Harassment
description: Even in these unimaginative days, Americans are still capable of innovations in cruelty.
author: Quinta Jurecic

title: The Capital Is a Mess
description: Chain-link fences, construction cranes, armed guards, and portable toilets everywhere
author: Matt Viser

title: American History as Rorschach Test
description: A new curriculum plays down America’s sins.
author: Hanna Rosin

title: What Would Mark Twain Think of America at 250?
description: The country’s greatest satirist loved technology and hated corruption. He’d have plenty to say about both in 2026.
author: Ron Chernow

title: Why Everyone Is Suddenly Talking About ‘Universal Basic Capital’
description: The policy could provide a much-needed hedge against a future AI dystopia—but only if it’s designed the right way.
author: Rogé Karma

title: A Supreme Court Decision That Might Improve Politics
description: The recent campaign-finance ruling is actually good. Or at least, it’s not bad.
author: David A. Graham

title: So Long to America’s Favorite Everymensch
description: The internet couldn’t get enough of “Freddy,” a German tourist on an epic World Cup road trip. Why did he suddenly go silent?
author: Will Oremus

title: I Went to the Great American State Fair and I May Never Sleep Again
description: A day at the fair featuring patriotic energy drinks, a George Washington impersonator, and lots of ABBA
author: Alexandra Petri

title: Something Is Happening in the Democratic Base
description: Voters are over moderates and incumbents.
author: Russell BermanandElaine Godfrey

title: The Supreme Court’s Utterly Mainstream Ruling on Women’s Sports
description: The justices position themselves at the center of American public opinion.
author: Helen Lewis

title: The Joy of Being American
description: Photos of July 4 celebrations across the decades
author: Alan Taylor

title: The American Experiment Is Worth Believing In
description: The Atlantic’s editor in chief, Jeffrey Goldberg, on America’s 250th anniversary and the American idea today. Plus: what we can learn from American history on this Fourth of July andCity on a Hill: A
author: David Frum

title: Putin Is Slipping Into Delusion
description: The Russian dictator remains obsessed with his war in Ukraine but doesn’t seem to comprehend how badly it’s going.
author: Simon Shuster

title: There’s Nothing Democratic About These Socialists
description: The DSA was formed in opposition to the very thing it has become.
author: Jonathan Chait

title: Young Republican Activists Are Turning Against Trump
description: Conservative groups at colleges across the country want a far more radical GOP.
author: Charlie Sabgir

title: Trump’s Anti-Patriotic Trap
description: The president celebrates only some Americans. But patriotism is not about who is in the Oval Office.
author: Anne Applebaum

title: The Vultures Arrived Before the Rescue Teams
description: Venezuela’s government has managed to make a natural disaster even worse.

title: The Kennedy Center’s Latest Defense Raises a New Mystery
description: What is the Trump Kennedy Center Foundation? And why is it being mentioned only now?
